title: Role Mapping
lede: Stop configuring permissions one member at a time. Role Mapping links each Contact Center role to an Access role and a View profile — so the moment someone is made an Agent, Supervisor, Monitor or Admin, the platform hands them exactly the right permissions automatically.

steps.title: Open the Role Mapping tab
steps.content:
Role Mapping lives inside a group's settings in the Contact Center workspace:

  1. In the left sidebar, under CALL CENTER, click Contact Center.
  2. At the top of the page, open the Groups tab.
  3. In the Groups panel on the left, click the group you want to configure — its settings open on the right.
  4. In the group's inner tab bar, click Role Mapping.
💡 Don't see the Role Mapping tab? It only appears for the main account or a sub-account with user management enabled. A Contact Center Admin who is not a user manager sees the other group tabs but not this one — the mapping is set for them by a User-Management user.

The same tab is also available from User Management → Groups — it's the exact same form, so you can edit the mapping from whichever page you're on.

images.alt: Contact Center Groups tab with the Role Mapping tab open for a group
images.caption: Role Mapping: the allowed lists on top, the role-to-access mapping below.

images.alt: Contact Center Groups tab with the Role Mapping tab open for a group
images.caption: Role Mapping: the allowed lists on top, the role-to-access mapping below.

images.alt: Group inner tab bar with the Role Mapping tab selected
images.caption: The group's inner tab bar — Role Mapping sits between Members Roles and Inbound SIP Routing.

images.alt: Group inner tab bar with the Role Mapping tab selected
images.caption: The group's inner tab bar — Role Mapping sits between Members Roles and Inbound SIP Routing.

steps.title: Understand the three pieces being connected
steps.content:
Role Mapping ties together three separate concepts — knowing what each one does makes the rest of this page obvious:

  • Contact Center role — what a member can see and do inside the Contact Center. There are four:
    • Agent — a regular rep who makes and receives calls and handles customers. Sees only their own queue.
    • Monitor / QA — can listen to calls and review reporting and recordings, but cannot manage agents or change settings.
    • Supervisor — manages agents: listen, whisper, barge, force status, review performance.
    • Admin — full Contact Center access, including the Groups and Call Transfer tabs.
  • Access role — the platform-wide permission role from User Management (what tables and actions the user may touch across the whole app).
  • View profile — which pages and menus the user actually sees (their simplified view of the app).

A member's effective Contact Center role is the highest role they hold across all their groups, and the main account is always Admin. Role Mapping's job: when a member's Contact Center role is set, automatically assign the matching Access role and View profile — no manual permission work per member.

steps.title: Choose the allowed Access roles and View profiles
steps.content:
The first card, Allowed access roles & view profiles, is a safety boundary. It decides which values may be used in the mapping below:

  1. In Access roles, select every access role this group's mapping should be allowed to hand out.
  2. In View profiles, select every view profile the mapping may assign.

Both fields are multi-select — click into the field and pick as many entries as you need; click the × on a tag to remove it.

⚠️ These lists bound what a Contact Center Admin can assign to this group's members — an empty list means a CC Admin can assign nothing. They do not limit you as a User-Management user: you can still assign any role or profile directly in User Management. The limit applies only to the Contact Center mapping.

images.alt: Allowed access roles and view profiles card with multi-select fields
images.caption: The allow-lists bound which values the mapping dropdowns below can use.

images.alt: Allowed access roles and view profiles card with multi-select fields
images.caption: The allow-lists bound which values the mapping dropdowns below can use.

steps.title: Map each Contact Center role
steps.content:
Now the heart of the feature — the Contact Center role → Access role & View profile card. It shows one row per Contact Center role: Agent, Monitor / QA, Supervisor and Admin.

  1. For each role you want to automate, pick an Access role in the first dropdown.
  2. Pick a View profile in the second dropdown.
  3. Repeat for every row you care about — you don't have to fill them all.
  • The dropdowns only offer values you added to the allow-lists in the previous step. If both dropdowns look empty, the card reminds you: "Add some allowed access roles / view profiles above to map them here."
  • An empty cell means "don't touch" — members with that Contact Center role keep whatever Access role or View profile they already have. The mapping never clears an existing value.
  • Use the × (clear) icon on a dropdown to empty a cell again.
💡 A classic setup: map Agent to a locked-down access role and a minimal view profile (dialer + their queue only), and map Supervisor to a broader role with reporting pages. New hires then get a production-ready workspace the second you set their role.

images.alt: Mapping grid with one row per Contact Center role and dropdowns for access role and view profile
images.caption: One row per role. Empty cells leave members of that role unchanged.

images.alt: Mapping grid with one row per Contact Center role and dropdowns for access role and view profile
images.caption: One row per role. Empty cells leave members of that role unchanged.

steps.title: Save — and watch the mapping apply itself
steps.content:
Click Save in the bottom-right corner. One click does two things:

  1. Saves the allow-lists and the mapping itself.
  2. Immediately applies the mapping to every member of the group, based on the Contact Center role each member currently has on the Members Roles tab.

The rules the platform follows when applying:

  • A member with no explicit Contact Center role counts as an Agent.
  • The main account is never remapped — its access can't be accidentally downgraded.
  • Empty mapping cells never clear a member's existing Access role or View profile.
  • Members whose values already match are skipped — nothing is rewritten needlessly.
  • If any member can't be updated, you'll see a warning like "2 member(s) could not be updated to the role mapping." — the rest are still applied.

steps.title: Verify the result on the Members Roles tab
steps.content:
Switch to the Members Roles tab to see the mapping in action:

  1. Each member row shows their editable Contact Center role dropdown.
  2. Next to it, the Access role (by role) and View profile (by role) columns show what the mapping assigns — these columns are read-only, because they're derived from the mapping, not set per member.
  3. Change a member's Contact Center role and click Save — their Access role and View profile follow the new role automatically.
💡 The main account's columns show Main account, and an unmapped role shows Not mapped. If a column shows the wrong target, fix it once on the Role Mapping tab — not per member.

images.alt: Members Roles tab showing the read-only Access role and View profile columns derived from the mapping
images.caption: Members Roles: the Access role / View profile columns are derived from the Role Mapping and apply on Save.

images.alt: Members Roles tab showing the read-only Access role and View profile columns derived from the mapping
images.caption: Members Roles: the Access role / View profile columns are derived from the Role Mapping and apply on Save.

steps.title: New users follow the mapping automatically
steps.content:
The mapping isn't just for existing members. When you create a user from inside a group (the Create User action on the group's Members tab), you pick their Contact Center role in the creation form — and the new account's Access role and View profile follow the group's Role Mapping from the very first login.

That's the payoff: onboarding a new agent becomes create user → pick role → done. No trip to User Management, no permission checklist, no forgotten settings.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: The mapping is live — now set each member's role and put the group to work on real calls.

metaDescription: Map each Contact Center role to an Access role and View profile in iMash so group members automatically receive the right permissions when their role is set.
